title: puignant french pork shoulder quiche
description: a crispy savory quiche filled with pork shoulder, onions, and beurre-monte, perfect for kids and adults alike, with a flaky crust that shines like a glassy-jelly
instructions: roll out the dough to fit a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom
trim the edges of the dough and press it into the pan
in a large skillet, cook the pork shoulder steaks over medium-high heat until browned on both sides, about 5 minutes per side
add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes
add the flour to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly
add the beurre-monte and cook until the mixture is smooth and slightly thickened, stirring constantly
in a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs, heavy cream, grated cheese, salt, and pepper
pour the egg mixture over the cooked pork and onion mixture in the skillet
spread the chopped thyme over the top of the egg mixture
pour the egg mixture into the baked tart crust
bake the quiche for 35-40 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the center is set
remove the quiche from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
